texto: I don't know what religious people do. I kind of wished I'd been a Christian with the blind faith that God is doing the right thing. As a Buddhist, you feel like you have more control over the situation, and that you can change your karma.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marcia Wallace: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't know what religious people do. I kind of wished I'd been a Christian with the blind faith…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, change, god pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e god.

frase_original: I don't know what religious people do. I kind of wished I'd been a Christian with the blind faith that God is doing the right thing. As a Buddhist, you feel like you have more control over the situation, and that you can change your karma.
